title: Creating a table with duplicate column name should not be possible
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE products (id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(99) NOT NULL, price DECIMAL(7,2) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE products_duplicate (id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, name1 VARCHAR(99) NOT NULL, name2 VARCHAR(99) NOT NULL, price DECIMAL(7,2) NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO products (id, name, price) VALUES (1, 'apple', 0.99);
INSERT INTO products_duplicate (id, name1, name2, price) VALUES (2, 'orange', 'orange', 1.29);
SELECT id, name1, price FROM products_duplicate;
key_bug_features: Creating tables with duplicate column names allowed
